Import patient-level graph features as a SummarizedExperiment

Source: R/importGraphFeatures.R
importGraphFeatures.Rd

Reads the pre-computed patient-level cell-graph feature matrix (CSV) and returns it as a SummarizedExperiment ready for multi-modal downstream analysis.

Usage

importGraphFeatures(fname, colData = NULL, feature_groups = TRUE)

Arguments

fname

Character(1). Path to the CSV file of patient-level graph features. Expected columns: patient_id plus one column per feature.

colData

A data.frame or DataFrame with additional sample-level annotations (rows must be patient IDs). NULL to skip.

feature_groups

Logical(1). If TRUE (default), annotate each feature with its thematic group in rowData.

Value

A SummarizedExperiment object with:

assay “graph_features”

numeric matrix of dimension features \(\times\) patients.

colData

patient ID plus any additional annotations from colData.

rowData

feature name and, when feature_groups = TRUE, a feature_group column classifying each feature into one of 14 thematic groups (see .classify_graph_features).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
csv_file <- system.file(
    "extdata", "example_graph_features.csv",
    package = "CellGraphR"
)
graph_se <- importGraphFeatures(csv_file)
dim(graph_se)       # features x patients
rowData(graph_se)   # feature annotations with group labels
} # }
